rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion 1. To Recall And Revoke 4/29/20 Order; 2. To Dissolve The Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 15, RESPONDENT NELLY ALLEN Motion 1. To Recall And Revoke 4/29/20 Order; 2. To Dissolve The Injunction. Respondent Nelly Allen Gabueva's motion to recall and revoke the court's April 29, 2020 order is denied. On June 29, 2020, Respondent filed a notice of appeal from that order. However, the order "is not appealable and shall not be stayed by petition for a writ, except as ordered by the superior court or by the appellate court." (Bus. & Prof. Code § 6126.3(l).) The court declines to revisit or stay its order, for at least two reasons. First, Respondent's motion amounts to a motion for reconsideration, which must be denied as untimely. (See Code Civ. Proc. § 1008(a) [motion for reconsideration must be filed within 10 days after service of notice of entry of order].) Further, to the extent that the motion purports to be based on new or different facts, Respondent fails to "provide a satisfactory explanation for failing to present the evidence sooner." (California Correctional Peace Officers Ass'n v. Virga (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 30, 47, fn. 15; see also Garcia v. Hejmadi (1997) 58 Cal.App.4th 674, 690 ["strict requirement of diligence"].) Second, Respondent's motion lacks any colorable factual or legal basis. As Respondent admits, she is "not admitted to the State Bar" of California. (Gabueva Decl., par. 5.) The court's April 29, 2020 order granting the Bar's application for assumption of jurisdiction over Respondent's unauthorized law practice was based on substantial-indeed, overwhelming-evidence submitted by the State Bar that Respondent nevertheless was "advertising or holding herself out as practicing or entitled to practice law in California, and [was] otherwise practicing law without authority." (Order, 3.) Respondent's "affidavit" in support of her motion, which consists largely of incoherent legal argument, does not rebut or even address any of that evidence, and in any event is unsworn and therefore inadmissible. (See Code Civ. Proc. § 2015.5.) Entry of this court's order was warranted in order to avoid prejudice to the interests of clients or other interested persons or entities affected by Respondent's unauthorized practice of law. (See Bus. & Prof. Code §§ 6125, 6126(a), 6126.3, and 6030.) As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the State Bar is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Permission To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 4, PLAINTIFF TONY FU Motion For Permission To File First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Tony Fu's motion for permission to file first amended complaint is granted. There is a strong policy in favor of liberal allowance of amendments to pleadings. (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) If Defendants wish to challenge the sufficiency of the new causes of action on the ground that they are uncertain or time-barred, the appropriate mechanism for doing so is by way of a demurrer or dispositive motion. (See California Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1985) 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 281 ["the preferable practice would be to permit the amendment and allow the parties to test its legal sufficiency by demurrer, motion for judgment on the pleadings or other appropriate proceedings."].) There is no showing that Defendants would be prejudiced by the amendment. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DANMUI KAO ET AL VS. BLUE & GOLD FLEET, L.P. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 5, DEFENDANT BLUE & GOLD FLEET, L.P. Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement. Defendant Blue & Gold Fleet, L.P.'s motion to enforce settlement agreement is granted. In plaintiffs' counsel's declaration "acknowledging" the motion, counsel represents that his clients are aware of the motion and of the general release required by the settlement agreement, but that they have not executed the release or given him any instructions regarding the motion. Accordingly, the motion is unopposed, and will be granted. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and a separate judgment pursuant to the terms of the settlement agreement, and must email them to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: PREMISE DATA CORPORATION A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. STEFFEN WEISS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Second Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Moorea Brega To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PREMISE DATA CORPORATIO, A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Second Amended Motion To Compel Moorea Brega To Produce Documents Pursuant To Subpoena And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED. Third party Moorea Brega to produce all responsive documents within her possession, custody or control no later than 15 days from entry of order. Each party's request for sanctions is DENIED. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PREMISE DATA CORPORATION A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. STEFFEN WEISS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice And Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PREMISE DATA CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Amended Motion To Compel. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED. The deposition questions at issue are relevant and a plausible line of inquiry. The defendant's answers - whether deliberately or not - were non-responsive to the key component of the question(s) namely, the timing of the onset of the elements of his emotional distress. He continually answered the question with the substance - or, the "what" - of statements made to his therapist, but never the "when" insofar as the timing of the onset of those symptoms. Therefore, counsel's objections and instructions were improper and the plaintiff is entitled to full and complete response. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PREMISE DATA CORPORATION A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. STEFFEN WEISS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Production Of Materials And Testimony For Which Steffen Weiss Waived A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PREMISE DATA CORPORATION, A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Production Of Materials And Testimony For Which Steffen Weiss Waived Attorney-Client Privilege. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. The defendant's refusal to answer questions related to the objective facts of his deposition preparation was unfounded and he must answer those questions. The defendant similarly is required to answer all questions directed at learning whether, in preparation for deposition, he reviewed documents that refreshed his recollection regarding topics of the deposition. Those documents, if any, must be produced. Additionally, the defendant waived attorney-client privilege as to specific disclosures made in text messages to Amy Wright. The defendant must answer questions specifically focused on those disclosures. To the extent the plaintiff seeks to broaden the attorney-client privilege waiver beyond the specific text messages sent to Ms. Wright, the motion is DENIED. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NAVVAB TADJVAR VS. MICHAEL KROUSE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Response And Production And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 3, DEFENDANT MICHAEL KROUSE'S Amended Motion To Compel Response And Production And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is GRANTED. The plaintiff's written responses, dated March 12, 2020, were unverified and not considered answers at all. (Appleton v. Superior Court (1988) 206 Cal.App.3d 632, 635-36.) Those responses also failed to comply with Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.230 in that they failed to specify the reason for any stated inability to produce the requested documents. Verified, code compliant responses to be served no later than 10 days after entry of order. The defendant's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $2,627.50, payable within 10 days of entry of order. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@ndllegal.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BRISTOL SL HOLDINGS, INC. ET AL VS. UNITED BEHAVIORAL HEALTH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Plaintiff Bristol Sl Holdings Motion To Strike Defendant'S First Affirmative Defense For Recoupment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 10, PLAINTIFF BRISTOL SL HOLDINGS, INC., CEDAR CREEK RECOVERY, INC. Motion To Strike Defendant's First Affirmative Defense For Recoupment. Plaintiff Bristol SL Holdings' motion to strike defendant United Behavioral Health's first affirmative defense is denied. Bristol fails to establish that the standard warranting a motion to strike has been met. (Code Civ. Proc. § 436; see PH II, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683 [use of motion to strike should be "cautious and sparing"].) To the extent that there are factual disputes regarding the bases for the defense, they cannot be decided on a motion to strike. (Full tentative ruling emailed to the parties). rulings.Case Title: BENJAMIN MCCANN ET AL VS. ALYSON FRIEDENSOHN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel With Correct Hearing Date
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 4, PLAINTIFFS BENJAMIN MCCANN AND ERIK BUCHANAN'S Motion To Compel With Correct Hearing Date (Part 1 of 2 for purposes of entry of tentative ruling). Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. While the plaintiff generally is correct that the absence of an operative complaint due to demurrer being sustained with leave to amend does not excuse a defendant from responding to discovery, the interrogatories at issue are each specifically drafted by reference to "allegations in the Complaint." As phrased, they are unintelligible and cannot be answered without an operative complaint in place. Therefore, the motion in regard to Special Interrogatory Nos. 2-6 is DENIED. In contrast, the plaintiff's request for production of documents are not so framed. The only opposition raised by the defendant to this motion is the lack of operative complaint. That argument cannot form the basis of a refusal to respond to discovery in these circumstances. (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arthur Young & Co. (1990) 223 Cal.App.3d 1429, 1436 n. 3 [citing Budget Finance Plan v. Superior Court (1973) 34 Cal.App.3d 794, 797; Union Mutual Life Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 1, 12.].) Therefore, the motion to compel responses to plaintiff's request for production of documents is GRANTED. Verified responses to be served within 10 days of entry of order. Each party's request for sanctions is DENIED. (Tentative Ruling continues in Part 2 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE GASTELUM ET AL VS. LYFT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 12, Motion For Change Of Venue. Defendant Pantoja's motion to change venue to San Diego County based upon the convenience of witnesses and ends of justice is denied. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 397, subd. (c).) Defendant fails to meet his burden as the moving party. "The burden rests upon one who seeks a change of venue under [Code of Civ. Proc. § 397(c)] to prove that both the convenience of witnesses and the ends of justice will be promoted thereby. This he must do through affidavits" that "set forth the names of the witnesses, the nature of the testimony expected from each, and the reasons why the attendance of each would be inconvenient." (Corfee v. S. Cal. Edison Co. (1962) 202 Cal.App.2d 473, 477.) Defendant fails to provide any such factual support for its motion, such as providing declarations of named witnesses. (See Juneau v. Juneau (1941) 45 Cal.App.3d 14; see also Braunstein v. Superior Court In and For Monterey County (1964) 225 Cal.App.3d 691, 698 [mere conclusions do not satisfy the Juneau rule].) Defendant only provides counsel's declaration and, in its reply brief, the names of two investigating officials. This is not sufficient evidence to meet defendant's burden. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion To Seal Pertaining To Pltf'S Opposition To Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 13, PLAINTIFF JANE DOE MOTION / Motion To Seal Pertaining To Plaintiff's Opposition To Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens. Grant. No opposition filed, and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Counsel for the movant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JANE DOE VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens Brought By Uber Technologies, Inc. And Rasier, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, August 3, 2020, Line 13, DEFENDANT UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC., RAISIER, LLC Motion To Stay Or Dismiss Based On Forum Non Conveniens Brought By Uber Technologies, Inc. And Rasier, LLC. Defendants Uber Technologies and Raiser's motion to dismiss based on forum non conveniens is denied. A corporate defendant's principal place of business is presumptively a convenient forum. (National Football League v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 902, 917.) Further, where the plaintiff is a California resident, the plaintiff's choice of a forum should rarely be disturbed unless the balance of public and private interests is strongly in favor of the defendant. (Id.; see Roman v. Liberty University, Inc. (2008) 162 Cal.App.4th 670, 684 ["Plaintiff is a resident of California, and substantial weight must be given to [her] choice of forum in this state."].) (Full tentative ruling emailed to the parties). As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
